description:
Second half of High F2 (performance). Consume the F2a index to run, per mutant, only the test modules whose covered line-set intersects the mutated line — instead of re-running the full cited suite for every one of S sites.

Trigger: any run citing a suite larger than a few tests. Blast radius today: S*N test executions where O(S*k) is achievable; on 200 sites x 500 tests that is ~100k vs a few thousand — 1-2 orders of magnitude, growing linearly with suite size.

Files: lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:947 (mutation input — thread the index in), lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:736, lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex:201 (register/run only covering modules).

## Approach
Per site: covering = index[site.file][site.line] (MapSet of test modules). site_test_modules = filter cfg.test_modules to covering. Register/run only those. On EMPTY/missing index for a line -> FALL BACK to all cited modules (safety: never under-test a mutant).

## Done
- Each mutant runs only its covering test modules, with empty-index -> all-tests fallback.
- A correctness test proves kill/survive classification is IDENTICAL to the full-suite baseline on a fixture where narrowing is non-trivial.
- (Optional) telemetry/log of tests-run-per-site for the perf win to be observable.
title: F2b: Narrow test_modules per site from the coverage index (O(S*N) -> O(S*k))
